GDPR:
1. The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is a set of rules that businesses in the European Union (EU) must follow when they collect and use personal information from people. This includes things like names, email addresses, and medical records.

2. If your healthcare business is located in Delaware (DE) and deals with any personal information of people in the EU, you need to follow the GDPR rules. This is because the GDPR applies to all businesses that collect or use data from people in the EU, even if the business is not located in the EU.

3. The most important thing to do to stay compliant with the GDPR is to have a clear process for how you collect, use, and protect personal data. This includes getting permission from people before using their data and having a plan to quickly notify people if there is a data breach.
NIS2:
1. This regulation is a new European Union (EU) law that focuses on improving cybersecurity across important industries, including healthcare. It requires companies to report any major cyberattacks or incidents within a specific timeframe and to have certain security measures in place.

2. If your healthcare business is located in Delaware (DE) and operates in the EU, this regulation is important because it applies to you. A successful cyberattack on your company could disrupt patient care and lead to hefty fines if you don't report it or meet the security requirements.

3. The single most important thing to do to stay compliant is to review the regulation carefully, identify any gaps in your current cybersecurity practices, and make a plan to address them as soon as possible. This could involve training your staff, upgrading your software and systems, and establishing clear incident response procedures.
AI Act:
1. The EU AI Act is a new set of rules that govern the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in Europe. It divides AI systems into different risk levels, from those that are completely banned to those that have less strict requirements.

2. This regulation matters for your healthcare business in Delaware because it applies to all companies that use AI, even if they are not based in the EU. If your company uses any AI systems, you need to make sure they comply with the new rules.

3. The single most important thing to do to stay compliant is to carefully review all of the AI systems your company uses and make sure they fall into the right risk category. You may need to make changes to your AI systems or the way you use them to meet the new requirements.
PSD2:
1. This regulation is about how payments are handled in the European Union (EU). It requires extra security checks for certain payments, forces banks to share information with other payment providers, and says that businesses need to be approved to offer payment services.

2. This matters for your healthcare business in Delaware (DE) because you likely accept payments from patients and other EU-based customers. You need to make sure your payment systems meet the new security and authorization requirements.

3. The single most important thing to do to stay compliant is to work closely with your payment processor and IT team to implement the strong customer authentication (SCA) procedures required by the regulation. This will help protect your business and your customers from fraud.
AML:
1. This regulation is about making sure that businesses, like banks, crypto companies, lawyers, accountants, and real estate agents, follow certain rules to prevent money laundering. It requires them to do background checks on their customers, report any suspicious transactions, and keep records for 5 years.

2. As a healthcare business in Delaware, this regulation matters because it applies to you if you accept payments or handle money in any way. Even if you don't think your business is at risk of money laundering, you still need to follow these rules.

3. The single most important thing to do to stay compliant is to have a strong "Know Your Customer" (KYC) process in place. This means you need to thoroughly verify the identity and background of your customers before doing business with them.
DORA:
1. The Digital Operational Resilience Act is a new European Union (EU) regulation that focuses on making sure financial companies, including healthcare businesses, have strong and reliable information technology (IT) systems. It requires these companies to have a plan to manage IT-related risks and to report any major IT problems.

2. Even though your healthcare business is based in Delaware, not the EU, this regulation still applies to you because you likely work with EU-based financial institutions. This means you need to make sure your IT systems are secure and can withstand any disruptions.

3. The most important thing to do to stay compliant with this regulation is to review and update your IT risk management plan regularly, and to test your IT systems to make sure they are working properly and can handle any problems that may come up.
